# Pull Request Template for awesome-java

## For submitting Throttle to awesome-java list

**Repository**: https://github.com/akullpp/awesome-java

**Section**: Bean Mapping or Concurrency

### PR Title:
```
Add Throttle - Resource-aware task executor
```

### PR Description:
```markdown
## Project Name
Throttle

## Category
Concurrency

## Description
Resource-aware task executor with automatic pause/resume based on CPU/memory usage. Features priority scheduling, chunk-based execution, and zero external dependencies.

## Links
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/sdeonvacation/throttle
- **Maven Central**: https://central.sonatype.com/artifact/io.github.sdeonvacation/throttle
- **License**: Apache 2.0

## Why it should be included
- Solves a common problem: preventing background tasks from overwhelming applications
- Production-ready with comprehensive tests and documentation
- Active maintenance and community engagement
- Available on Maven Central
- Unique approach with checkpoint-driven resource monitoring

## Checklist
- [x] Project is at least 30 days old
- [x] README is in English
- [x] Project has a clear license (Apache 2.0)
- [x] Project is actively maintained
- [x] Project has tests
- [x] Project has documentation
- [x] Project is available on Maven Central
```

### Code to Add:
Find the "Concurrency" section and add:

```markdown
* [Throttle](https://github.com/sdeonvacation/throttle) - Resource-aware task executor with automatic pause/resume based on CPU/memory usage.
```

---

## Alternative: Submit to awesome-spring-boot

**Repository**: https://github.com/ityouknow/awesome-spring-boot

### Code to Add:
Find a relevant section (e.g., "Background Jobs" or "Utilities") and add:

```markdown
* [Throttle](https://github.com/sdeonvacation/throttle) - Intelligent task executor for Spring Boot with automatic resource management and pause/resume capabilities.
```

---

## Tips for Successful Submission:

1. ✅ Make sure your project meets all criteria
2. ✅ Follow the list's formatting exactly
3. ✅ Place alphabetically within the section
4. ✅ Keep description concise (one line)
5. ✅ Be patient - reviews can take a few days
6. ✅ Be responsive to feedback
7. ✅ Don't be discouraged if rejected - improve and resubmit

## When to Submit:
- After getting 10-20 GitHub stars (shows community interest)
- After 30 days of project existence
- When documentation is solid
- When tests are passing
